nodes.name: Factory + marketing review samples
nodes.documentation: Factory (fit/function/material) + marketing (look/positioning) review the samples. Decision dropdown {approve-into-flow | rework}. [SCOR S1.6/PLM | source: AB NPD review]

nodes.name: Factory + marketing review
nodes.documentation: Branch A. Factory checks dieline/fit; marketing checks branding/positioning. Expression for marketing engagement: isNewProduct == true -> marketing reviews; else factory only. Decision {approve | corrections}. [SCOR S1.10 | source: AB factory+marketing, marketing only if new]

nodes.name: Quality review (English + structure)
nodes.documentation: Branch B. Quality verifies English text accuracy, mandatory declarations, barcodes, dimensions, and structural correctness of the artwork. Decision {approve | corrections}. [SCOR S1.10 | ISO 8.6 | source: AB quality artwork review]

nodes.name: Language reviewer (Arabic text)
nodes.documentation: Branch C. The language reviewer verifies the Arabic text (translation accuracy, script, right-to-left layout). Decision {approve | corrections}. A separate specialist lane because Arabic correctness is a distinct competency. [SCOR S1.10 | source: AB Arabic language reviewer]

nodes.name: Collate corrections; request revised proof
nodes.documentation: Buyer collates the corrections from every reviewer into one markup and requests a revised proof from the supplier. The revised proof re-enters the parallel review (revised-proof loop). [SCOR S1.10 | source: AB revised-proof loop]

nodes.name: Release to print; store last-approved artwork
nodes.documentation: System releases the approved artwork to print and stores it as item.lastApprovedArtworkId (with version + approval record) so a future reorder can take the last-approved-artwork shortcut. Audit + SSE. The item then proceeds into the normal procurement/PO flow. [SCOR S1.10/OE4 | source: AB print release + last-approved store]

nodes.name: Factory requests RM + artwork samples
nodes.documentation: Buyer/factory requests raw-material samples and first-cut artwork samples from the supplier for the new product. Captures sampleType {RM|artwork}, quantity, target spec. [SCOR S1.6/PLM | source: AB NPD sample request]

nodes.name: Supplier sends samples
nodes.documentation: Supplier sends the RM + artwork samples for evaluation. [SCOR S1.6 | source: AB]

nodes.name: Forward customer artwork to supplier to print
nodes.documentation: Customer-supplied print-ready artwork is sent to the supplier to print directly. No internal proof loop. [SCOR S1.10 | source: AB customer artwork direct]

nodes.name: Supplier submits proof (1-up / 2-up / 10-up)
nodes.documentation: For own-brand artwork the supplier submits a proof at the agreed imposition: 1-up (single), 2-up, or 10-up (multiple repeats per sheet, dropdown {1-up|2-up|10-up}). The proof enters the parallel internal review. [SCOR S1.10 | source: AB proof 1-up/2-up/10-up]

nodes.name: New product development needed?
nodes.documentation: Expression: isNewProduct == true (a product not previously made). True -> run the NPD sample loop first (RM + artwork samples reviewed by factory + marketing). False -> go straight to the artwork decision for an existing product. [SCOR S1.1 | source: AB NPD]

nodes.name: Samples approved?
nodes.documentation: Expression: sampleDecision == approve. True -> the new product enters the normal flow and proceeds to the artwork decision. False -> rework: loop back to request revised samples from the supplier. [SCOR S1.6 | source: AB sample loop]

nodes.name: Item is printed?
nodes.documentation: Expression: item.isPrinted == true (label/carton/sleeve carrying artwork). True -> artwork approval path. False (unprinted item) -> no artwork needed, exit to normal procurement. [SCOR S1.1 | source: AB printed-item]

nodes.name: Reorder of last-approved artwork?
nodes.documentation: Expression: item.lastApprovedArtworkId != null AND noArtworkChange == true. True -> SHORTCUT: reuse the last-approved artwork, skip the proof + review loop, print directly (reorder). False -> new or changed artwork needs proofing. [SCOR S1.10 | source: AB last-approved-artwork reorder shortcut]

nodes.name: Customer-supplied or own-brand artwork?
nodes.documentation: Expression: artworkOrigin == customer-supplied. customer-supplied -> the customer's print-ready artwork is forwarded to the supplier to print DIRECTLY (no internal proof review; the customer owns it). own-brand -> the supplier submits a proof for internal parallel review. [SCOR S1.10 | source: AB customer vs own-brand artwork]

nodes.name: All reviewers approved?
nodes.documentation: Expression: reviewA == approve AND reviewB == approve AND reviewC == approve. True -> release to print. False -> any branch raised corrections; collate corrections and loop back to the supplier for a revised proof. [SCOR S1.10 | source: AB all-approved gate]

nodes.name: Parallel proof review
nodes.documentation: Parallel split: the proof is reviewed simultaneously by (A) factory + marketing [marketing engaged ONLY if the product is new], (B) quality [English text + structural correctness], and (C) the language reviewer [Arabic text]. All three branches must complete and approve before print. [SCOR S1.10/PLM | source: AB parallel artwork review]

nodes.name: Join: all reviews returned
nodes.documentation: Parallel join: waits for all engaged branches (A, B, C) to return. Then evaluate whether ALL approved. [SCOR S1.10 | source: AB parallel join]

nodes.name: New product / printed item need
nodes.documentation: Trigger: a need for a new product (NPD) and/or a printed packaging item (label, carton, sleeve). Two intertwined loops follow: the NPD sample loop (raw-material + artwork samples) and the artwork-proof approval loop. [SCOR S1.1/PLM | source: AB NPD + artwork]

nodes.name: Artwork approved / printed; into normal flow
nodes.documentation: NPD samples approved (if applicable) and artwork approved/printed (customer-direct, reorder shortcut, or full parallel review). The item joins the normal sourcing/PO cycle. [SCOR S1.10 | source: AB]
